Question 2111: What is the cardinal symptom of Bell’s palsy?
Bilateral facial weakness
Unilateral facial paralysis
Leg paralysis
Vision loss
Question 2112: What is the most important nursing intervention for Bell’s palsy?
Restrict fluids
Strict bedrest
Isolation precautions
Protect the affected eye
Question 2113: What is the cardinal symptom of trigeminal neuralgia?
Sudden, severe facial pain
Dull facial ache
Facial weakness
Vesicular rash
Question 2114: What medication class is most commonly used to treat trigeminal neuralgia?
Antibiotics
NSAIDs
Anticonvulsants
Antihistamines
Question 2115: What is the cardinal symptom of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis?
Sensory deficits
Dementia
Progressive muscle weakness
Tremor
Question 2116: What nursing priority is most important for patients with ALS?
Pain management
Maintain airway and respiratory function
Fluid restriction
Isolation precautions
Question 2117: What is the cardinal symptom of Huntington’s disease?
Rigidity
Flaccid paralysis
Sensory loss
Choreiform movements
Question 2118: What is the priority nursing intervention for Huntington’s disease?
Ensure patient safety
Strict bedrest
Isolation precautions
Fluid restriction
Question 2119: What is the cardinal symptom of cluster headaches?
Severe unilateral periorbital pain
Bilateral headache
Visual aura
Dull ache
Question 2120: What is the most effective acute treatment for cluster headaches?
NSAIDs
Antibiotics
High-flow oxygen
Sedatives
Question 2121: What is the cardinal symptom of migraine headache?
Bilateral tight head pain
Periorbital stabbing pain
Occipital ache only
Unilateral throbbing pain with photophobia
Question 2122: What is the most appropriate acute treatment for migraine headache?
Antibiotics
Triptans
High-flow oxygen
Sedatives
Question 2123: What is the cardinal symptom of tension headache?
Throbbing unilateral pain
Visual aura
Bilateral band-like pressure
Periorbital stabbing pain
Question 2124: What is the most appropriate treatment for tension headache?
Analgesics and stress reduction
Triptans
Oxygen therapy
Antibiotics
Question 2125: What is the cardinal symptom of subarachnoid hemorrhage?
Gradual headache onset
Sudden severe “worst headache of life”
Mild frontal headache
Scalp tenderness
Question 2126: What is the priority nursing action for suspected subarachnoid hemorrhage?
Encourage ambulation
Encourage oral intake
Encourage exercise
Maintain strict bedrest
Question 2127: What is the cardinal symptom of spinal cord compression?
Acute confusion
Chest pain
Headache
Progressive motor weakness
Question 2128: What is the priority intervention for spinal cord compression?
Encourage ambulation
Immediate immobilization
Apply heat
Massage therapy
Question 2129: What is the cardinal symptom of autonomic dysreflexia?
Hypotension
Isolated bradycardia
Sudden severe hypertension
Fever
Question 2130: What is the first nursing action in autonomic dysreflexia?
Raise the head of the bed
Place patient supine
Administer IV fluids
Administer sedatives
Question 2131: What is the most common trigger of autonomic dysreflexia?
Pain
Bladder distention
Infection
Hypoglycemia
Question 2132: What is the cardinal symptom of epilepsy?
Single seizure episode
Chronic headaches
Progressive memory loss
Recurrent unprovoked seizures
Question 2133: What is the priority nursing intervention during an active seizure?
Protect the patient from injury
Apply restraints
Insert oral airway
Give oral medications
Question 2134: What is the cardinal symptom of status epilepticus?
Single brief seizure
Visual aura only
Continuous seizure activity without recovery
Postictal confusion
Question 2135: What is the priority treatment for status epilepticus?
Oral anticonvulsants
Antibiotics
IV benzodiazepines
Analgesics
Question 2136: What is the cardinal symptom of delirium tremens?
Bradycardia
Tremors, agitation, and hallucinations
Hypothermia
Excessive sedation
Question 2137: What is the priority treatment for delirium tremens?
Antipsychotics
Antibiotics
Analgesics
Benzodiazepines
Question 2138: What is the cardinal symptom of Wernicke’s encephalopathy?
Confusion, ataxia, ophthalmoplegia
Hypertension
Isolated memory loss
Tremor
Question 2139: What is the priority treatment for Wernicke’s encephalopathy?
IV thiamine
IV glucose first
Antibiotics
Sedatives
Question 2140: What is the most important nursing consideration when administering IV thiamine?
Administer as rapid IV push
Give orally only
Administer before glucose
Timing is not important
Question 2141: What is the cardinal symptom of Guillain-Barré syndrome?
Descending paralysis
Isolated sensory loss
Spastic paralysis
Ascending muscle weakness
Question 2142: What is the priority nursing concern in Guillain-Barré syndrome?
Pain control
Respiratory function
Skin breakdown
Nutritional intake
Question 2143: What is the cardinal symptom of myasthenia gravis?
Muscle rigidity
Sensory deficits
Muscle weakness that improves with rest
Choreiform movements
Question 2144: What nursing intervention is most important for myasthenia gravis?
Schedule activities after rest periods
Encourage continuous activity
Restrict fluids
Isolation precautions
Question 2145: What is the cardinal symptom of multiple sclerosis?
Continuous neurologic decline
Relapsing and remitting neurologic deficits
Isolated motor paralysis
Loss of consciousness
Question 2146: What nursing intervention is most important for multiple sclerosis?
Encourage excessive exercise
Restrict fluids
Isolation precautions
Prevent fatigue and overheating
Question 2147: What is the cardinal symptom of a transient ischemic attack?
Permanent neurologic deficits
Progressive memory loss
Loss of consciousness
Temporary neurologic deficits
Question 2148: What is the priority nursing action for suspected TIA?
Observe at home
Treat as a medical emergency
Encourage exercise
Administer sedatives
Question 2149: What is the cardinal symptom of hydrocephalus?
Peripheral edema
Chest pain
Increased intracranial pressure
Isolated bradycardia
Question 2150: What is the priority nursing assessment for a patient with a ventriculoperitoneal shunt?
Signs of increased intracranial pressure
Abdominal distention
Peripheral edema
Skin turgor
Question 2151: What is the cardinal symptom of increased ICP in infants?
Sunken fontanelle
Bulging fontanelle
Hypotonia
Peripheral cyanosis
Question 2152: What is the cardinal symptom of brain tumor?
Intermittent mild headache
Peripheral edema
Chest pain
Morning headache with vomiting
Question 2153: What nursing intervention is most important for a patient with brain tumor?
Monitor neurologic status frequently
Keep patient flat
Provide excessive stimulation
Encourage excessive fluids
Question 2154: What is the cardinal symptom of narcolepsy?
Insomnia
Night terrors
Sudden uncontrollable sleep attacks
Sleep apnea
Question 2155: What is the most important nursing teaching for narcolepsy?
Avoid all daytime sleep
Drive without restrictions
Schedule planned daytime naps
Use caffeine only
Question 2156: What is the cardinal symptom of sleep apnea?
Nightmares
Recurrent episodes of apnea during sleep
Difficulty falling asleep
Sleepwalking
Question 2157: What is the most effective treatment for obstructive sleep apnea?
Sedative medications
Oxygen therapy only
Antibiotics
CPAP therapy
Question 2158: What is the cardinal symptom of increased ICP in adults?
Decreased level of consciousness
Hypotension
Diarrhea
Peripheral neuropathy
Question 2159: What nursing intervention helps reduce intracranial pressure?
Avoid hip flexion
Encourage coughing
Trendelenburg positioning
Encourage excess fluids
Question 2160: What is the most important discharge teaching for a patient with seizure disorder?
Stop medications when seizures stop
Resume driving immediately
Take anticonvulsants consistently
Consume alcohol freely
Question 2161: What is the earliest clinical sign of hypoxia?
Cyanosis
Bradycardia
Hypotension
Restlessness
Question 2162: What is the most reliable indicator of adequate oxygenation?
Skin color
Pulse oximetry
Respiratory rate
Blood pressure
Question 2163: What oxygen delivery device provides the highest concentration of oxygen?
Nasal cannula
Simple face mask
Non-rebreather mask
Venturi mask
Question 2164: What is the primary purpose of a Venturi mask?
Deliver precise oxygen concentrations
Deliver maximum oxygen
Provide humidification only
Emergency airway management
Question 2165: Which condition requires cautious oxygen administration?
Asthma
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
Pneumonia
Pulmonary edema
Question 2166: What is the normal oxygen saturation range for a healthy adult?
85–90%
100–105%
90–94%
95–100%
Question 2167: What is the priority nursing action for a patient with sudden respiratory distress?
Document findings
Administer medications
Offer oral fluids
Assess airway and breathing
Question 2168: Which finding indicates worsening respiratory failure?
Mild tachypnea
Decreased level of consciousness
Anxiety
Accessory muscle use
Question 2169: What breathing pattern is associated with metabolic acidosis?
Shallow respirations
Irregular respirations
Kussmaul respirations
Apnea
Question 2170: What is the primary goal of oxygen therapy?
Maintain adequate tissue oxygenation
Cure respiratory disease
Remove carbon dioxide
Replace mechanical ventilation
Question 2171: Which position best promotes lung expansion?
Supine position
High Fowler’s position
Trendelenburg position
Prone position
Question 2172: What is the most common complication of prolonged oxygen therapy?
Hypertension
Hypoglycemia
Renal failure
Oxygen toxicity
Question 2173: What is the best method to prevent atelectasis?
Incentive spirometry
Prolonged bedrest
Fluid restriction
Sedation
Question 2174: Which assessment finding suggests airway obstruction?
Clear breath sounds
Crackles
Stridor
Productive cough
Question 2175: What is the priority action when stridor is present?
Offer oral fluids
Reposition only
Prepare for airway management
Document findings
Question 2176: Which intervention helps mobilize pulmonary secretions?
Prolonged bedrest
Chest physiotherapy
Fluid restriction
Sedation
Question 2177: What is the best indicator of effective coughing?
Loud cough
Facial flushing
Increased dyspnea
Clear breath sounds after coughing
Question 2178: What is the primary purpose of suctioning?
Remove airway secretions
Provide oxygen
Treat infection
Replace ventilation
Question 2179: Which action reduces the risk of hypoxia during suctioning?
Pre-oxygenate the patient
Prolong suction time
Place patient supine
Increase suction pressure
Question 2180: What assessment finding indicates the need for suctioning?
Clear breath sounds
Dry cough
Gurgling breath sounds
Normal SpO₂
Question 2181: What is the earliest sign of airway obstruction?
Cyanosis
Bradycardia
Hypotension
Restlessness
Question 2182: Which finding indicates ineffective gas exchange?
Pink skin coloration
Decreased oxygen saturation
Normal respiratory rate
Normal blood pressure
Question 2183: Which oxygen delivery device is best for a patient requiring precise FiO₂?
Nasal cannula
Simple face mask
Venturi mask
Non-rebreather mask
Question 2184: Which condition commonly causes hypoventilation?
Central nervous system depression
Anxiety
Fever
Physical exertion
Question 2185: Which assessment finding suggests respiratory acidosis?
Kussmaul respirations
Hypoventilation
Tachypnea
Hyperventilation
Question 2186: What is the priority action when oxygen saturation drops below 90%?
Document the finding
Offer oral intake
Encourage ambulation
Increase oxygen delivery
Question 2187: Which breath sound indicates fluid in the alveoli?
Wheezes
Stridor
Pleural rub
Crackles
Question 2188: Which nursing intervention reduces the work of breathing?
Supine positioning
Upright positioning
Trendelenburg position
Flat positioning
Question 2189: What is the primary indication for mechanical ventilation?
Mild hypoxia
Stable COPD
Inability to maintain airway or ventilation
Anxiety
Question 2190: Which assessment finding indicates ventilator-associated pneumonia?
Fever and purulent secretions
Clear sputum
Afebrile status
Dry cough
Question 2191: Which intervention reduces the risk of ventilator-associated pneumonia?
Keep patient supine
Elevate head of bed 30–45°
Daily ventilator circuit changes
Deep continuous sedation
Question 2192: Which ABG finding indicates respiratory alkalosis?
Elevated PaCO₂
Decreased pH
Normal ABG values
Decreased PaCO₂
Question 2193: Which condition commonly causes respiratory alkalosis?
Anxiety
Sedation
Opioid overdose
Neuromuscular disease
Question 2194: What is the priority nursing action for hyperventilation?
Administer high-flow oxygen
Encourage activity
Encourage slow, controlled breathing
Administer sedatives
Question 2195: Which finding indicates carbon dioxide retention?
Tachypnea
Hypotension
Decreased level of consciousness
Peripheral cyanosis
Question 2196: Which nursing action helps prevent aspiration?
Lying flat after meals
Elevating the head of the bed during feeding
Rapid feeding
Large-volume bolus feeding
Question 2197: Which assessment finding suggests aspiration pneumonia?
Clear breath sounds
Afebrile status
Dry cough
Crackles with fever after feeding
Question 2198: Which patient is at highest risk for aspiration?
Patient with decreased level of consciousness
Alert ambulatory patient
Young healthy adult
Independent eater
Question 2199: What is the priority intervention for suspected aspiration?
Suction the airway
Offer fluids
Continue feeding
Document the event
Question 2200: Which outcome best indicates effective respiratory nursing care?
Ongoing shortness of breath
Continued accessory muscle use
Oxygen saturation maintained above 95%
Persistent crackles
Question 2201: Which assessment finding is an early indicator of respiratory compromise?
Cyanosis
Bradycardia
Hypotension
Anxiety
Question 2202: Which position is most appropriate for a patient experiencing dyspnea?
Supine position
Upright sitting position
Trendelenburg position
Flat positioning
Question 2203: Which oxygen delivery device is appropriate for long-term low-flow oxygen therapy?
Non-rebreather mask
Venturi mask
Nasal cannula
Simple face mask
Question 2204: Which assessment finding indicates effective oxygen therapy?
Improved oxygen saturation
Persistent tachypnea
Cyanosis
Accessory muscle use
Question 2205: Which patient is most at risk for hypoventilation?
Anxious patient
Patient receiving opioid analgesics
Patient exercising
Patient with fever
Question 2206: Which finding suggests respiratory muscle fatigue?
Normal respiratory rate
Occasional cough
Clear lung sounds
Paradoxical breathing
Question 2207: What is the priority intervention for a patient with ineffective airway clearance?
Administer sedatives
Restrict fluids
Enforce bedrest
Encourage coughing and deep breathing
Question 2208: Which breath sound is associated with bronchoconstriction?
Crackles
Wheezes
Stridor
Pleural friction rub
Question 2209: Which intervention is most effective for relieving bronchospasm?
Antibiotics
Antitussives
Bronchodilators
Analgesics
Question 2210: Which assessment finding suggests pulmonary edema?
Pink, frothy sputum
Dry cough
Clear lung sounds
Bradycardia
Question 2211: What is the priority nursing action for acute pulmonary edema?
Place patient supine
Position patient upright
Administer IV fluids
Encourage ambulation
Question 2212: Which ABG finding indicates metabolic acidosis?
Elevated pH
Decreased PaCO₂
Normal ABG values
Decreased pH
Question 2213: Which condition commonly causes metabolic acidosis?
Diabetic ketoacidosis
Vomiting
Hyperventilation
Antacid overuse
Question 2214: What respiratory pattern compensates for metabolic acidosis?
Shallow respirations
Irregular respirations
Kussmaul respirations
Apnea
Question 2215: Which nursing action helps reduce oxygen demand?
Encourage frequent ambulation
Maintain cool environment
Cluster nursing care
Promote anxiety
Question 2216: Which sign suggests impending respiratory failure?
Mild tachypnea
Altered level of consciousness
Occasional cough
Clear lung sounds
Question 2217: Which intervention helps prevent ventilator-associated pneumonia?
Keep patient supine
Daily ventilator circuit changes
Continuous sedation
Regular oral care
Question 2218: Which assessment finding indicates readiness for weaning from mechanical ventilation?
Stable vital signs and adequate oxygenation
High oxygen requirements
Altered mental status
Frequent suctioning
Question 2219: What is the priority nursing action during ventilator weaning?
Monitor respiratory status closely
Ignore signs of fatigue
Remove ventilator abruptly
Increase sedation
Question 2220: Which outcome best indicates effective respiratory nursing management?
Continued shortness of breath
Ongoing accessory muscle use
Stable oxygen saturation within target range
Persistently abnormal ABGs
Question 2221: Which assessment finding suggests ineffective airway clearance?
Clear breath sounds
Normal respiratory rate
Pink skin color
Gurgling breath sounds
Question 2222: What is the priority intervention for ineffective airway clearance?
Administer sedatives
Suction the airway
Restrict fluids
Enforce bedrest
Question 2223: Which assessment finding is most concerning in a patient receiving oxygen therapy?
Dry nasal passages
Mild headache
Decreased level of consciousness
Warm skin
Question 2224: Which oxygen delivery device is best for short-term high-flow oxygen?
Non-rebreather mask
Nasal cannula
Venturi mask
Simple face mask
Question 2225: Which position is contraindicated in severe respiratory distress?
Upright sitting
Supine position
High Fowler’s position
Tripod position
Question 2226: Which assessment finding suggests pulmonary embolism?
Crackles
Gradual shortness of breath
Productive cough
Sudden onset dyspnea and chest pain
Question 2227: What is the priority nursing action for suspected pulmonary embolism?
Encourage ambulation
Administer IV fluids
Observe without intervention
Administer oxygen
Question 2228: Which patient is at highest risk for developing a pulmonary embolism?
Ambulatory patient
Postoperative patient with prolonged immobility
Young athlete
Outpatient surgical patient
Question 2229: Which finding suggests pleural effusion?
Wheezes
Stridor
Decreased breath sounds on one side
Crackles
Question 2230: What is the priority nursing intervention for a large pleural effusion?
Prepare for thoracentesis
Place patient flat
Administer antitussives
Administer IV fluids
Question 2231: Which assessment finding suggests pneumothorax?
Crackles
Sudden unilateral absence of breath sounds
Productive cough
Wheezes
Question 2232: What is the priority nursing action for a suspected pneumothorax?
Encourage ambulation
Observe for improvement
Offer oral intake
Apply oxygen and notify provider immediately
Question 2233: Which complication is most concerning with tension pneumothorax?
Cardiovascular collapse
Mild shortness of breath
Dry cough
Nasal congestion
Question 2234: Which assessment finding suggests acute respiratory distress syndrome?
Mild dyspnea
Normal ABGs
Severe hypoxemia refractory to oxygen therapy
Productive cough
Question 2235: What is the priority nursing focus for a patient with ARDS?
Nutritional intake
Early ambulation
Oxygenation and ventilation support
Aggressive fluid administration
Question 2236: Which intervention helps improve oxygenation in ARDS?
Supine positioning
Prone positioning
Aggressive IV fluids
Low PEEP ventilation
Question 2237: Which assessment finding indicates worsening ARDS?
Improving SpO₂
Stable ABGs
Decreased respiratory effort
Increasing oxygen requirements
Question 2238: Which complication is associated with prolonged mechanical ventilation?
Ventilator-associated pneumonia
Hypertension
Hypoglycemia
Renal calculi
Question 2239: Which nursing action reduces the risk of barotrauma during ventilation?
Monitor peak airway pressures
Increase tidal volume
Silence ventilator alarms
Keep patient supine
Question 2240: Which outcome best indicates effective management of acute respiratory failure?
Ongoing hypoxemia
Continued accessory muscle use
Adequate oxygenation with decreased work of breathing
Increasing PaCO₂
Question 2241: What is the best intervention to promote skin integrity for a patient with diarrhea?
Leave the area open to air
Cleanse with alcohol wipes
Scrub the skin vigorously
Cleanse with mild soap and warm water, and apply a barrier cream
Question 2242: What is the best way to perform an assessment on an infant/child who is crying and/or frightened?
Ask the parent to leave the room
Perform the assessment as the mother holds or is touching the child and reassure them that everything will be ok because their mother will not leave them alone
Complete the assessment quickly without explanation
Delay reassurance until the end
Question 2243: What is the best intervention for a nurse who is working on a unit AFTER orientation but still feels uncomfortable working on said unit?
Request reassignment
Avoid asking questions
Ask a senior nurse for mentorship
Continue without support
Question 2244: What is the best suggestion to promote safety in the home environment?
Suggest removing all hazards a child would encounter. Wandering occurs in the late stages of Alzheimer’s Dementia and is the primary cause of un-witnessed accidents in the home environment
Increase supervision only
Lock medications only
Install door alarms only
Question 2245: What is the best response to a patient concerned with a family issue?
Give reassurance without discussion
“You seem concerned with this issue about your family, would you like to discuss it?”
Offer immediate solutions
Redirect the conversation
Question 2246: What is the best intervention when dealing with recently diagnosed adolescents between the ages of 13 – 15?
Encourage independence only
Limit discussion to parents
Provide written materials only
Provide peer support as much as possible. Organization of peer discussion is the most effective intervention
Question 2247: What is the best intervention when dealing with a patient who has mental health issues and is engaging in multiple sexual relationships simultaneously?
Advise abstinence only
Take no action
Warn the patient of consequences
Teach the patient about sexual intercourse protection, supply the patient with contraception if available, and advise the mental health physician of the patients behavior
Question 2248: What is the best intervention AFTER a family member has been taken to a quiet place and is informed of the nurses role in an emergency situation?
Allow them time alone
Ensure that a staff member stays with the family member
Provide written information
Bring them back immediately
Question 2249: What is the most common SOCIAL detriment impeding plans to produce new establishments related to healthcare (e.g. hospice etc.)
Staffing shortages
Zoning laws
Financial instability and support
Lack of technology
Question 2250: What is the best intervention to provide care if facility policy is not available?
Refer to the Professional Standards of Practice set forth by the province of licensure. Following this ensures protection of license in the event of a mistake being made
Use personal judgment only
Follow peer instruction
Delay care
Question 2251: What is the priority nursing intervention for a patient experiencing acute confusion?
Administer sedatives
Ensure patient safety and reduce environmental stimuli
Provide reality orientation only
Order diagnostic tests immediately
Question 2252: Which action best promotes autonomy when caring for an elderly patient?
Completing tasks for the patient
Enforcing strict routines
Allowing family to decide everything
Encouraging the patient to participate in care decisions
Question 2253: What is the most important nursing action when a patient refuses treatment?
Assess the patient’s understanding and capacity
Proceed with treatment
Document refusal only
Discharge the patient
Question 2254: Which finding indicates effective pain management?
Stable vital signs
Patient sleeping continuously
Patient reports acceptable pain level
Increased mobility
Question 2255: What is the priority intervention for a patient at risk of falls?
Apply physical restraints
Increase monitoring only
Implement fall-prevention strategies
Review medications later
Question 2256: Which nursing action best supports a patient experiencing grief?
Provide reassurance only
Encourage verbalization of feelings
Change the subject
Set expectations for recovery
Question 2257: What is the best nursing response to a medication error?
Conceal the error
Document without reporting
Wait for symptoms to appear
Report the error according to policy
Question 2258: Which factor most increases the risk of infection?
Compromised immune system
Advanced age
Gender
Height
Question 2259: What is the primary goal of palliative care?
Improve quality of life
Cure the disease
Prolong life at all costs
Withdraw all treatment
Question 2260: Which nursing action demonstrates advocacy?
Remaining silent
Carrying out all orders
Speaking up for the patient’s rights
Delegating concerns
Question 2261: What is the priority nursing intervention when a patient reports chest pain?
Administer pain medication first
Prepare for diagnostic testing
Document the complaint
Assess the patient and obtain vital signs immediately
Question 2262: Which intervention best supports infection control?
Wearing gloves only
Performing hand hygiene before and after patient contact
Isolating all patients
Administering prophylactic antibiotics
Question 2263: What is the best nursing response to a patient expressing anger?
Defend the care provided
Ignore the behavior
Acknowledge the patient’s feelings and encourage expression
Set strict limits immediately
Question 2264: Which action best demonstrates therapeutic communication?
Using open-ended questions
Asking yes-or-no questions
Giving personal advice
Redirecting the conversation
Question 2265: What is the priority concern for a patient with impaired mobility?
Nutritional intake
Risk for injury
Sleep pattern disturbance
Social isolation
Question 2266: Which nursing action best promotes patient dignity?
Completing care quickly
Making decisions without the patient
Enforcing identical routines
Respecting privacy and personal preferences
Question 2267: What is the priority nursing action for a patient with nausea and vomiting?
Administer antiemetics immediately
Modify diet
Document symptoms
Assess hydration status
Question 2268: Which factor most influences patient adherence to treatment?
Age
Understanding of the treatment plan
Gender
Medical diagnosis
Question 2269: What is the nurse’s primary role in ethical decision-making?
Making decisions independently
Avoiding ethical issues
Advocating for the patient
Following orders without question
Question 2270: Which outcome best indicates effective nursing care?
Patient goals are met safely
Patient follows instructions
Patient satisfaction only
All tasks completed
Question 2271: What is the nurse’s priority when initiating a new medication?
Administer the medication immediately
Verify the medication order and patient identity
Document administration first
Provide patient teaching only
Question 2272: Which nursing action best prevents pressure injuries?
Keeping skin moist at all times
Massaging reddened areas
Increasing calorie intake only
Repositioning the patient regularly
Question 2273: What is the most appropriate nursing response to patient anxiety?
Encourage the patient to verbalize concerns
Provide reassurance only
Change the subject
Administer sedatives immediately
Question 2274: Which finding requires immediate nursing intervention?
Mild postoperative pain
Temperature of 37.8°C
Sudden drop in blood pressure
Reduced appetite
Question 2275: What is the priority nursing action for a patient with hypoglycemia?
Administer insulin
Encourage physical activity
Administer a fast-acting carbohydrate
Document findings only
Question 2276: Which intervention best supports effective communication?
Using medical terminology only
Adjusting language to the patient’s level of understanding
Providing pamphlets only
Speaking quickly
Question 2277: What is the nurse’s priority when caring for a confused patient?
Provide reality orientation only
Administer sedatives
Order tests immediately
Maintain patient safety
Question 2278: Which action best promotes patient compliance?
Providing clear explanations and rationale
Using fear-based education
Assuming understanding
Giving complex instructions
Question 2279: Which nursing intervention reduces aspiration risk?
Positioning the patient upright during meals
Feeding the patient lying flat
Offering large bites
Encouraging conversation while eating
Question 2280: What is the priority nursing action for dehydration?
Administer diuretics
Restrict fluids
Encourage fluid replacement
Promote activity
Question 2281: Which nursing action best supports patient education?
Teach immediately
Provide excessive information
Speak quickly
Assess readiness to learn
Question 2282: What is the nurse’s priority when a patient experiences dizziness?
Encourage walking
Assist the patient to a safe position
Order tests immediately
Document only
Question 2283: Which intervention best promotes sleep?
Encourage television use
Offer caffeine
Reduce noise and lighting
Schedule frequent checks
Question 2284: Which action best demonstrates professionalism?
Maintaining patient confidentiality
Discussing patient issues publicly
Sharing personal opinions
Skipping protocols
Question 2285: What is the priority nursing action when a patient faints?
Assist the patient to stand
Ensure airway and patient safety
Offer food immediately
Document only
Question 2286: Which nursing action best reduces medication errors?
Relying on memory
Administering medications quickly
Multitasking during administration
Following the rights of medication administration
Question 2287: What is the nurse’s priority when a patient reports shortness of breath?
Administer medication immediately
Prepare for tests
Document the complaint
Assess airway and breathing
Question 2288: Which nursing action best supports coping?
Minimize patient concerns
Encourage use of support systems
Promote isolation
Avoid discussion
Question 2289: What is the nurse’s responsibility when informed consent is required?
Obtain the consent personally
Proceed without consent
Verify that consent has been obtained and is understood
Rely on family consent
Question 2290: Which outcome best indicates quality nursing care?
Patient outcomes achieved safely
All tasks completed
Patient satisfaction only
Patient compliance
Question 2291: What is the nurse’s priority action when a patient suddenly becomes unresponsive?
Document the event
Assess airway, breathing, and circulation
Notify the family
Administer medications
Question 2292: Which nursing intervention best prevents hospital-acquired infections?
Wearing gloves only
Prophylactic antibiotics
Isolating all patients
Proper hand hygiene
Question 2293: What is the nurse’s priority when caring for a patient with impaired swallowing?
Prevent aspiration
Ensure adequate nutrition
Encourage fluids
Provide education
Question 2294: Which nursing action best supports ethical practice?
Ignoring patient wishes
Acting for convenience
Respecting patient autonomy
Deferring all decisions to family
Question 2295: What is the priority nursing intervention for fever?
Administer antibiotics
Apply ice packs immediately
Assess the patient and monitor temperature trends
Document findings only
Question 2296: Which action best promotes patient safety during ambulation?
Encourage fast walking
Use appropriate assistive devices
Allow unassisted ambulation
Ignore footwear
Question 2297: What is the nurse’s priority when a patient reports new pain?
Administer analgesics immediately
Document the complaint
Provide reassurance only
Assess the pain characteristics
Question 2298: Which nursing action best supports patient-centered care?
Incorporating patient preferences into care
Providing identical care to all patients
Focusing only on efficiency
Following policy without discussion
Question 2299: What is the nurse’s responsibility when a patient expresses fear?
Encourage expression and provide support
Minimize the fear
Change the subject
Provide reassurance only
Question 2300: Which outcome best indicates effective nursing care?
All tasks completed
Patient satisfaction only
Patient goals met safely
Patient compliance
